task.title: Add docs Q&A/readme explainer mode for repo docs folder.

context.problem_statement: Developers need a local way to ask what the repo docs and policy files say before waiving docs work, requesting a docs patch, or escalating review. The kit has docs-impact and docs-propose signals, but no deterministic local explainer/Q&A bridge for README and docs policy context.
background: AGW-019 added docs-propose sidecar artifacts for missing docs coverage.
AGW-022 and AGW-023 added docs-contract PR comments and a spec-only slash-command grammar.
AGW-024 added a read-only changelog-update helper for release-note proposal/check mode.
AGW-025 should borrow the useful shape of Qodo /ask or codebase chat without requiring hosted PR bots or uncited model answers.
non_goals: Do not implement hosted PR comment execution or GitHub API mutation.
Do not generate uncited free-form answers as authoritative repo policy.
Do not write docs, waivers, task packets, VERSION, or CHANGELOG.md by default.
Do not replace docs-impact, docs-propose, or explicit task-packet approval before write-capable docs changes.
Do not add a dependency on a hosted model or network call.

report_sources: git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main for source and kit remotes
finalizer_receipt_paths: source main commit d86fd6dd55690391a35fbbf790190598b2b8a21a closed AGW-024
kit main commit aca8d0f2be2463755f3252d6ade79df542ed0692 completed repo-contract-kit 0.4.50
previous_task_state.notes: AGW-025 packet creation began only after AGW-024 was pushed in both repos, both remotes matched local HEADs, both worktrees were clean, source task-status reported zero active tasks, and agent-next selected AGW-025.

closeout_required_before_start.reason: The previous AGW-024 task has source and kit closeout commits, matching remote refs, clean worktrees, and no active task metadata.
closeout_required_before_start.required_next_step: Proceed with exactly one AGW-025 worker in repo-contract-kit; refresh task-status if source or kit state becomes dirty before worker launch.

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep repo-contract-kit as the install-layer guardrail provider that helps local agents and humans understand docs policy and generate reviewable evidence without mutating target repos by default.

area_contracts.purpose: Stable wrapper CLI for local read-only helper commands.
Classifies changed paths into docs-impact categories that the explainer should help humans understand.
Existing no-target-write docs proposal path that the explainer should route to when docs changes are needed.
Installed Make target surface for local docs explainer workflows.
Spec-only PR command policy for docs Q&A, waivers, add-docs, and read-only review boundaries.

stop_conditions: Stop if the helper would write target docs, waivers, task packets, VERSION, or CHANGELOG.md by default.
Stop if output invents policy answers without cited source paths.
Stop if the implementation requires hosted model, browser, account, or network access.
Stop if it weakens docs-impact, docs-propose, or waiver approval boundaries.

protected_files: Do not edit source prompt or schema truth in agent-workflow-kit for this kit-owned task.
Do not weaken docs-impact or docs-propose semantics.
Do not imply /waive-docs can be self-approved by an agent.
Do not add hosted PR bot behavior or network/model dependencies.
expected_outputs: A deterministic read-only docs explainer/Q&A helper with text and JSON output.
Question and optional focus/path inputs that select relevant README/docs/policy sources.
Grounding evidence with source paths, snippets or headings, and explicit no-match behavior.
A ready-to-use local prompt or explainer brief for a human/local agent.
Wrapper CLI and installed Make target exposure.
Docs that route users from explanation to docs-propose, waiver review, or scoped task-packet work.
VERSION and CHANGELOG.md record the repo-contract-kit behavior change.

acceptance_criteria.description: A local helper produces deterministic docs-explainer text/JSON output without writing target files or calling external services.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused helper tests assert no target writes, deterministic output, and no sidecar writes by default.

acceptance_criteria.description: The helper accepts a question and optional focus/path filters, selects relevant README/docs/policy files, and reports source paths or citations.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused helper tests cover question matching, no-match output, and focus/path filtering.

acceptance_criteria.description: Output includes a local prompt or explainer brief that can be handed to a local agent or human to answer docs-policy questions without inventing uncited policy.
acceptance_criteria.verification: JSON/text tests inspect prompt/brief fields and citation requirements.

acceptance_criteria.description: Wrapper CLI and installed Make target expose the helper while preserving non-mutating defaults.
Docs explain how the helper relates to docs-impact, docs-propose, waiver understanding, and add-docs/task-packet escalation.
acceptance_criteria.verification: docs-check, docs-freshness, and changed docs review.

acceptance_criteria.description: The behavior change is versioned and documented in repo-contract-kit.
Source backlog rows and summary close AGW-025 only after kit validation passes.

commands.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest tests.test_docs_explain tests.test_repo_contract_kit_cli tests.test_install

commands.expected_result: pass in source repo after closeout
pass in source repo after closeout
pass in source repo after closeout

readiness_check.expected_result: pass before lifecycle finish, or blocked with explicit receipt if validation cannot complete

lifecycle_action.expected_result: done metadata or explicit blocked finalizer receipt

final_task_status.expected_result: no active AGW-025 task metadata after parent closeout, or terminal metadata with linked receipt before cleanup

closeout_preview.expected_result: preview reports no unsafe dirty terminal worktree removal

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Worker must explain any remaining dirty files in the kit checkout; parent should only integrate expected AGW-025 files and leave both repos clean after commits.

docs_impact.reason: The task adds a local operator-facing command and installed workflow guidance.

known_risks: An explainer helper can look like an AI answer even when it only has partial docs context.
A waiver-help flow could accidentally imply that agents can approve waivers.
Broad docs scanning can become noisy or token-heavy if output is unbounded.
Hosted PR command language could imply behavior that is still only local/spec-only.
mitigations: Make output source-cited, bounded, and explicit about no-match or uncertainty cases.
Keep default behavior non-mutating and local-only.
Route write outcomes to docs-propose or task-packet approval instead of direct edits.
Document hosted command execution as future adapter work unless implemented.

approval.notes: User requested implementing remaining workflow-stack backlog items serially with one task agent per backlog item.

overlap_warnings: This task edits the companion kit checkout plus source backlog records; keep commits separate by repo.
coordination.worker_rule: Use exactly one task worker for this backlog item. The worker owns kit implementation files only; the parent owns source task-packet and backlog closeout.

next_packet_hint: After AGW-025, make agent-next should select the next open P2 item from the source backlog.
